observation: CoinDesk reported attackers exploited a pool bug to mint 1.28 billion aUSD, while follow-up coverage described about $3 billion of technically minted aUSD
market_health_use: Detect sudden stablecoin supply expansion without matching collateral or debt

observation: aUSD was reported to have depegged by roughly 99% after the erroneous minting event
market_health_use: Measure market confidence loss when supply integrity is uncertain

observation: Acala attributed the issue to a misconfiguration of the newly deployed iBTC/aUSD liquidity pool
market_health_use: Flag new pool launches as high-risk stablecoin accounting events

observation: CoinDesk follow-up coverage said the peg had partially recovered to about $0.80 after tracing and containment work
market_health_use: Track confidence recovery after erroneous supply is frozen, traced, or burned

observation: CoinDesk reported that the exploit involved a liquidity-pool configuration issue tied to the Honzon protocol despite audit history
market_health_use: Monitor deployment configuration and module permissions in addition to audited code
